title: Failure Classification and Retry Budgets · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for failure classification and retry budgets and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a typed Go failure and retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify wrapped Go failures with errors.Is and errors.As, then combine typed cause evidence with bounded attempt, age, delay, cancellation, and terminal disposition.
Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1340 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify failures and enforce attempt, age, delay, and terminal retry budgets; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify wrapped Go failures with errors.Is and errors.As, then combine typed cause evidence with bounded attempt, age, delay, cancellation, and terminal disposition.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Retry Observability Ownership
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Retry Observability Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3673 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify failures and enforce attempt, age, delay, and terminal retry budgets; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Retry Observability Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Queue depth alone explains whether retries are healthy. In Go, wrapping every failure as retryable improves resilience.
